//: Railway service config for a funny RUNNER (not the central server — that is railway.json). A runner connects OUT to a central server over the Socket.IO tunnel and executes agent work. This file is the source of truth for the published 'funny-runner' Railway template; point the runner service's 'Railway Config File' setting at it.
//start: Runs the runtime (packages/runtime) DIRECTLY — not bin/funny.js. In a full-repo build packages/server/dist also exists, and bin/funny.js would launch the central server; running the runtime entry guarantees runner mode. The runtime requires TEAM_SERVER_URL and, with no token/secret, performs device-link enrollment.
//worker: Outbound-only worker: with WS_TUNNEL_ONLY=true it dials the server and needs no inbound port — so there is intentionally NO healthcheck/domain. Railway keeps it up as long as the process runs.
//variables: Railway config-as-code can ONLY express build/deploy settings — NOT variables or volumes. Set in the template composer / service: (1) TEAM_SERVER_URL = the central server URL (the only required var; device-link delivers the bearer + forwarded-identity secret on approval, so NO RUNNER_AUTH_SECRET or invite token). (2) FUNNY_DATA_DIR = /data. (3) WS_TUNNEL_ONLY = true.
//volume: REQUIRED: attach a persistent volume mounted at /data (matching FUNNY_DATA_DIR). The runner stores its device-link credentials at $FUNNY_DATA_DIR/runner-credentials.json plus worktrees and scratch state; without a volume every redeploy wipes them and the runner must be re-enrolled (re-approved) each deploy.
